rationale: An axiom of T is defined as an element of the theory T, so the load-bearing prerequisite is the notion of a theory in the modest set-of-sentences sense (def:theory), not the deductive-closure def:axiom-systems-theory. The 'first-order language / Sent(L)' clause only locates where T lives; by the same test that treats A subset S as ambient, it is scaffolding, so the listed def:language is dropped.
licensing_quote: If $T\subseteq \operatorname{Sent}(\mathcal{L})$ is a theory, then an \emph{axiom of $T$} is an element $\alpha\in T$.
notes: Routing check: def:axiom-systems-theory is deductively closed, so under it every theorem would count as an axiom; the modest def:theory is the correct target. If a reviewer prefers to keep an explicit language locator, def:first-order-language is the precise node (the statement says 'first-order language'), superseding the more general def:language that was listed. Borderline removal -> moderate confidence by design.
